page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.medium is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.m2 server family. Built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, this instance features 4 dedicated physical cores with 4 vCPUs, operating with a single thread per core configuration. It is provisioned with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. The instance runs on the KVM hypervisor.

This instance does not include any bundled local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and application deployment. There are no integrated graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.m2.medium is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance local storage or GPU acceleration. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for hosting medium-sized databases, caching layers, in-memory data structures, and enterprise applications requiring a stable memory footprint without the overhead of hyperthreading.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.medium is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance within the ecs.m2 family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, operating with a single thread per core. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, but does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. Running on the KVM hypervisor, it delivers dedicated CPU resources without hyperthreading. This configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory caching, database hosting, and enterprise applications that require a stable memory footprint and consistent processing performance.
